newsSince: Hyundai Motor Group completed its acquisition of SoftBank's remaining ~9.65% stake in Boston Dynamics for ~$325M (board approval ~June 22), taking 100% ownership as Atlas moves to commercial deployment (Hyundai RMAC and Google DeepMind, fully committed for 2026). This modestly strengthens the humanoid-builder proof point but does not change Hyundai's character as a large, already-rerated auto stock. The -20% price move since June 9 is profit-taking/flow after a +114% YTD run, not a fundamental deterioration.

sources.label: Hyundai completes Boston Dynamics acquisition (Hyundai Newsroom)
Hyundai acquires SoftBank 9.6% Boston Dynamics stake (Seoul Economic Daily)

verdicts.rankRationale: Humanoid-stack lens: full 100% Boston Dynamics ownership (June 22) as Atlas commercializes is a genuine but small positive to the builder thesis. Still a $92B auto stock that has already rerated (+71% YTD), so prior 'down' bias holds - nudge score +1, no rank change. Atlas remains best humanoid proof point but exposure is indirect.
verdicts.bull: Now 100% owns Boston Dynamics; Atlas is a top humanoid proof point with committed 2026 deployments and a 30k-unit/yr US robotics plant planned.
verdicts.neutral: Humanoid is tiny vs the auto P&L; consolidation adds control but not near-term earnings.
verdicts.bear: Large, indirect, already-rerated auto stock; humanoid optionality diluted by auto cyclicality and tariff overhang.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Atlas commercial deployment scale-up / robotics plant ramp
verdicts.primaryRisk: Auto-cycle and tariffs dominate; humanoid value buried in a megacap
